items.reason: Meeting participation always starts with a verified browser join backend.
items.setup_hint: Use the meeting-browser-driver with a valid meeting URL.

items.reason: The runtime needs a virtual audio bus to listen to the meeting and route audio back if speaking is enabled.
items.setup_hint: Use BlackHole on macOS or PulseAudio null sink on Linux.

items.reason: Audio bus negotiation and device probing depend on ffmpeg.
items.setup_hint: Install ffmpeg before launching the meeting flow.

items.reason: The meeting flow listens first and extracts a transcript for follow-up work.
items.setup_hint: Set KYBERION_STT_COMMAND or use the stub transcript path for dry runs.

items.reason: This profile allows speaking, so a TTS path is required.
items.setup_hint: Set KYBERION_TTS_COMMAND and verify the chosen voice profile before joining.

items.reason: Speaking uses a promoted runtime voice profile.
items.setup_hint: Select an active voice profile id from the registry.

items.reason: Speaking into a meeting requires mission-scoped voice consent.
items.setup_hint: Create or verify evidence/voice-consent.json for the active mission.

items.reason: This meeting may benefit from visible participation, but camera is not mandatory unless explicitly requested.
items.setup_hint: Enable a virtual camera only if the meeting will be video-on.

questions: Will this meeting be video-on, or should Kyberion stay audio-only?
Will screen sharing or slides be needed?

tracking_expectations: Track all owner/deadline pairs until closed
notes: Stop before making final decisions unless explicitly authorized.
